title: Cerebral palsy - NHS
text:
al palsy can be difficult to cope with, which can lead to problems such as clinical depression  in some people Speak to your care team about the likely effects of cerebral palsy on you or your child.
Help and support If you or your child have been diagnosed with cerebral palsy, you may find it useful to contact a support group for information and advice.
Scope  is the main UK charity for people with cerebral palsy and their families. They offer:
an  online community a free telephone helpline: 0808 800 3333 (Monday to Friday 9am to 6pm, and Saturday and Sunday 10am to 6pm) an email helpline: helpline@scope.org.uk Find out more about:
advice and support from Scope for disabled people and their families support services from Scope Your care team may also be able to provide details of support groups in your local area.
Video: Cerebral palsy 
In this video, a cerebral palsy expert explains the causes, symptoms and treatment.
